question: The latest revision of the IRR of the Fire Code of the Philippines was released and became effective on what year?
keyMemory: Kapag Fire Code IRR revision year ang tinanong sa ALE context, tandaan mo: 2019.
explanation:
Basahin muna natin ang full question:
"The latest revision of the IRR of the Fire Code of the Philippines was released and became effective on what year?"
Choices:
a. 2017
b. 2018
c. 2019
d. 2020
Correct answer: c. 2019.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay 2019.
Ito ang gusto kong pumasok sa isip mo agad: Fire Code = RA 9514 = 2008 law, pero ang latest widely recognized revised IRR na ginagamit sa review at references ay 2019 Revised IRR. Wala ring lumabas sa mga source na mas bagong full revised IRR na pumalit dito, kaya sa ALE logic, 2019 ang expected answer. (Ecoglo)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Kasi ang tanong ay tungkol sa latest revision of the IRR, hindi sa original law.
Ang original Fire Code law is RA 9514 of 2008, pero ang revised IRR na tinutukoy ng reviewer ay iyong 2019 Revised IRR. Kaya huwag kang mahuhulog sa trap na "Fire Code is 2008, so baka 2008 ang sagot." Hindi iyon ang choices, at hindi iyon ang tanong. Ang tanong ay latest revision ng IRR. (Safety Signs PH)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. 2017 – masyadong maaga. Walang common ALE reference na nagsasabing 2017 ang latest revised IRR.
b. 2018 – same problem, parang filler choice lang ito para lituhin ka.
d. 2020 – ito ang medyo delikadong trap. Bakit? Kasi minsan iniisip ng examinee, "Baka 2019 na-release pero 2020 naging effective." Pero sa board-review framing, ang kilala at tinutukoy ay 2019 Revised IRR. Kaya ang inaasahang sagot sa ganitong tanong ay 2019, hindi 2020. (Ecoglo)
Elimination method, step by step
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Step 1: Hanapin ang main clue: "latest revision of the IRR of the Fire Code."
Ibig sabihin, hindi law year, hindi enactment year, kundi revision year ng IRR.
Step 2: I-eliminate ang mga obviously off timeline.
2017 at 2018 agad puwede mo nang pagdudahan, dahil ang Fire Code is 2008, at widely known sa reviewers na around a decade later nagkaroon ng malaking revision sa IRR.
Step 3: Pag natira ang 2019 at 2020, isipin mo:
Ano ang mas kilalang tawag sa document?
"2019 Revised IRR of RA 9514."
Ayun. Kaya 2019.
Keyword at trap word
Ang keyword dito ay: "latest revision" at "IRR."
Ang trap word naman ay implicit: baka isipin mo law year o effectivity year na hiwalay sa revision label. Sa ALE, madalas ang tamang sagot ay yung standard reference year ng code material mismo.
Philippine basis
RA 9514 – Fire Code of the Philippines of 2008
Latest board-review reference for revised IRR: 2019 Revised IRR (Safety Signs PH)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag Fire Code IRR revision year ang tinanong sa ALE context, tandaan mo: 2019.
________________________________________

question: The latest revision of the IRR of the National Building Code of the Philippines became effective on what year?
keyMemory: PD 1096 = 1977, pero latest revised IRR used in ALE context = 2005.
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"The latest revision of the IRR of the National Building Code of the Philippines became effective on what year?"
Choices:
a. 2005
b. 2008
c. 2019
d. 2023
Correct answer: a. 2005.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay 2005.
Ito, sobrang classic ALE memory item ito.
Ang National Building Code is PD 1096, year 1977, pero ang revised IRR na ginagamit at kinikilala ay the 2005 Revised Edition. At kahit may later memoranda at issuances, ang governing full revised IRR na nire-reference pa rin ay iyong 2005 RIRR, hindi 2019 at hindi 2023. (iibh.org)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Kasi ang tanong ulit ay hindi tungkol sa year ng PD 1096. Hindi rin ito tungkol sa ibang building-related law.
Ang tanong ay: latest revision of the IRR of the National Building Code.
Ang standard answer diyan sa Philippine review setting ay 2005 Revised IRR. Even newer DPWH/NBCDO issuances still refer to the existing 2005 RIRR, which tells you iyon pa rin ang base full revised IRR. (iibh.org)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
b. 2008 – trap ito. Bakit? Kasi 2008 is a very familiar year sa building laws dahil diyan pumasok ang RA 9514 Fire Code at BP 220 revised rules references. Pero hindi iyan ang latest revised IRR year ng NBCP.
c. 2019 – trap din ito dahil baka nadikit sa isip mo ang 2019 Fire Code IRR. Ito ang common board exam confusion: nalilito ang NBCP sa Fire Code.
d. 2023 – ito ang "modern-looking" distractor. Mukhang plausible kasi mas bago. Pero wala namang recognized full revised NBCP IRR na 2023 na pumalit sa 2005 sa sources na chineck. Kaya kapag ganito, huwag ma-intimidate sa mas bagong taon. Sa board exam, mas bago does not always mean tama. (iibh.org)
Elimination method, step by step
Ito ang reason bakit natitira ang 2005:
Step 1: Ihiwalay sa isip mo ang PD 1096 = 1977 versus its revised IRR = 2005.
Magkaiba iyon. Common trap iyan.
Step 2: Tanggalin ang 2019 agad kung kabisado mo na iyan ang Fire Code IRR zone.
Minsan ang exam gumagawa ng cross-confusion between laws.
Step 3: Tanggalin ang 2023 kung wala kang alam na major full revised NBCP IRR for that year.
Sa ALE, huwag basta pumili ng pinakabagong date kung wala kang legal/code anchor.
Step 4: Pag natira ang 2005 at 2008, isipin mo:
2008 belongs more naturally to Fire Code and some housing rules context.
So 2005 ang logical fit for NBCP revised IRR.
Keyword at trap word
Keyword: "National Building Code" at "IRR"
Trap: paghalo ng NBCP at Fire Code dates
Philippine basis
PD 1096 – National Building Code of the Philippines
Revised IRR / 2005 Revised Edition (iibh.org)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
PD 1096 = 1977, pero latest revised IRR used in ALE context = 2005.
________________________________________

question: What is not included in the coverage of the National Building Code?
keyMemory: Sa scope of NBCP, tandaan mo ang exception: BP 220 projects.
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"What is not included in the coverage of the National Building Code?"
Choices:
a. Public buildings
b. Open market housing of PD 957
c. BP 220 projects
d. Private buildings
Correct answer: c. BP 220 projects.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay BP 220 projects.
Ito, magandang tanong ito kasi hindi lang memory — scope and application ito.
Under the NBCP IRR, the Code applies to public and private buildings and structures, except traditional indigenous family dwellings and those covered by BP Blg. 220, meaning economic and socialized housing projects. Kaya sa choices, ang hindi kasama sa coverage ng NBCP ay BP 220 projects. (philarchitecturenotes.weebly.com)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Napakalakas ng clue sa tanong: "not included in the coverage."
Ang hanap mo dito ay iyong express exception sa NBCP.
At ang express exception nga ay those covered by BP 220. Hindi ito hula. Ito mismo ang standard scope reading sa NBCP IRR. (philarchitecturenotes.weebly.com)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Public buildings – mali ito kasi included ang public buildings sa coverage ng NBCP.
d. Private buildings – mali rin kasi included din ang private buildings.
Kaya kung nag-e-eliminate ka lang, actually malaki na chance mong makuha ang tamang sagot dahil parehong clearly included ang public at private buildings. (philarchitecturenotes.weebly.com)
b. Open market housing of PD 957 – ito ang mas tricky distractor. Bakit?
Kasi housing law din siya, so puwedeng malito ang examinee. Pero ang tanong is very specific: ano ang hindi kasama sa coverage ng NBCP?
Ang code exception na alam mo by text is BP 220 projects, hindi simpleng "open market housing of PD 957." So kahit mukhang housing-related at mukhang legal-sounding, hindi ito ang exact exclusion na hinahanap ng tanong. Ito ang classic exam trap: related, but not the exact code exception.
Elimination method, step by step
Ganito ang board strategy:
Step 1: Hanapin ang exact legal scope.
NBCP covers public and private buildings.
Step 2: Therefore, tanggal agad ang:
• public buildings
• private buildings
Kasi malinaw na kasama iyan.
Step 3: Natira:
• open market housing of PD 957
• BP 220 projects
Step 4: Alin ang explicitly exempted sa NBCP coverage?
BP 220 projects.
Ito ang reason bakit natitira ito sa choices.
Ito rin ang reason bakit natatanggal ang PD 957 option:
hindi siya ang exact exception phrase na ginagamit sa NBCP scope provision.
Keyword at trap word
Keyword: "not included", "coverage of the National Building Code"
Trap word: housing-related alternatives
Kapag parehong mukhang legal terms ang natira, piliin mo yung exact law or code explicitly named as exception.
Philippine basis
PD 1096 / NBCP IRR scope applies to public and private buildings and structures, except traditional dwellings and those covered by BP 220.
BP 220 governs economic and socialized housing projects. (philarchitecturenotes.weebly.com)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa scope of NBCP, tandaan mo ang exception: BP 220 projects.
________________________________________

question: Any physical change made on a building/structure to increase its value, utility and/or to improve its aesthetic quality?
keyMemory: Kapag ang tanong may words na value, utility, aesthetics, automatic mong isipin: Renovation.
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"Any physical change made on a building/structure to increase its value, utility and/or to improve its aesthetic quality?"
Walang visible choices sa page, pero based on the NBCP definition, the correct answer is:
Correct answer: Renovation.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay Renovation.
Ito ay definition-type question. Sa ALE, gusto nilang makita kung kabisado mo ang distinction ng mga terms gaya ng renovation, repair, alteration, addition, conversion. Sa NBCP/related building-law references, renovation ang tawag sa physical change made to improve value, utility/quality, or aesthetics. (iibh.org)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Pansinin mo ang wording:
"increase its value"
"utility"
"improve its aesthetic quality"
Iyan ang language ng renovation.
Kapag ang tanong ay tungkol sa pagpapaganda, pagpapataas ng gamit, pagtaas ng value, that is renovation thinking. Hindi lang simpleng ayos ng sira, hindi lang change of use, at hindi rin necessarily dagdag-floor-area.
Paano mo siya ihihiwalay sa ibang usual choices?
Kahit hindi visible ang choices dito, ito ang common confusions:
Renovation – physical improvement for value, utility, aesthetics.
Repair – pagbabalik sa original condition ng nasira o deteriorated.
Alteration – pagbabago sa configuration or components ng building, pero hindi laging emphasis ang aesthetics/value.
Addition – may idinadagdag na floor area, volume, or extension.
Conversion – change in use or occupancy.
Kaya kung ang tanong ay nakasentro sa pag-improve ng building, especially value and aesthetics, renovation ang best answer. (iibh.org)
Elimination method, step by step
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Step 1: Hanapin ang strongest clue words.
Dito ang strongest clues ay:
• increase value
• utility
• improve aesthetic quality
Step 2: Tanggalin ang "repair" kung choice siya.
Bakit? Repair is restoration.
Ang repair, ang goal ay ibalik sa original condition, hindi necessarily taasan ang value o pagandahin.
Step 3: Tanggalin ang "conversion" kung choice siya.
Bakit? Conversion is about change in use or occupancy, like residential to commercial.
Step 4: Tanggalin ang "addition" kung choice siya.
Bakit? Addition usually means dagdag area/volume.
Hindi iyon ang pinakamalinaw na clue dito.
Step 5: Kung may "alteration" at "renovation," piliin ang mas tumutugma sa words na value, utility, aesthetic quality.
That is renovation.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang common trap dito ay pinapapili ka between alteration at renovation.
Mukhang parehong pagbabago, oo. Pero kapag ang tanong ay naka-emphasize sa pag-improve ng value at aesthetics, mas tumuturo iyon sa renovation.
Philippine basis
NBCP/DPWH glossary-type references use Renovation for physical changes intended to improve value, utility/quality, and aesthetics. (iibh.org)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang tanong may words na value, utility, aesthetics, automatic mong isipin: Renovation.
________________________________________
Sige, tuloy tayo sa Page 2.
May continuation lang sa taas ng previous item. Yung choices na Alteration, Addition, Renovation, Conversion at yung definitions sa ilalim, they actually confirm na tama ang nauna nating sagot sa previous page: Renovation. Hindi ko na siya uulitin nang buo para hindi tayo paikot. Dito tayo magsisimula sa first complete new question sa page na ito.

question: As per BP 220, maintain a ratio for house and lot where __% of the selling price for a house and lot package is allotted for the house and __% for the lot?
keyMemory: Kapag BP 220 at tinanong ang house-and-lot selling price ratio, tandaan mo agad: 60% house, 40% lot.
explanation:
Basahin muna natin ang full question:
"As per BP 220, maintain a ratio for house and lot where __% of the selling price for a house and lot package is allotted for the house and __% for the lot?"
Choices:
a. 60% : 40%
b. 70% : 30%
c. 75% : 25%
d. 80% : 20%
Correct answer: a. 60% : 40%.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay 60% for the house, 40% for the lot.
Ito ang gusto kong pumasok agad sa isip mo: sa BP 220, may recognized parameter na ang lot portion should not exceed 40% of the maximum selling price of the house-and-lot package, kaya automatic ang balance niyan is 60% for the house and 40% for the lot. Kaya pag nakita mo ang choices na ito, dapat may code memory ka na agad: 60-40. (DHSUD)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang tanong dito ay hindi random market practice. Hindi ito "usually" or "commonly." Ang clue ay "As per BP 220". Ibig sabihin, code-based ratio ang hinahanap, hindi estimate, hindi developer preference. Since BP 220 economic and socialized housing standards recognize the 40% ceiling for the lot component of a house-and-lot package, ang house component therefore becomes 60%. Kaya ang tamang sagot ay 60% : 40%. (DHSUD)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
70 : 30 sounds neat, pero wala itong exact BP 220 anchor dito.
75 : 25 masyadong maliit ang lot share versus the rule being tested.
80 : 20 mas lalo nang extreme; parang sobra namang liit ng lot portion para sa standard na tinatanong.
Ang common trap dito, pipili ka ng "mas maganda pakinggan" na ratio. Pero sa ALE, kapag may phrase na "As per BP 220", huwag kang magre-rely sa instinct. Dapat hanapin mo yung exact remembered standard. (DHSUD)
Elimination method, step by step
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Una, keyword mo: "As per BP 220."
Ibig sabihin may exact parameter.
Pangalawa, hanapin mo ang number pair na parang codal memory item, hindi opinion.
Sa housing laws, madalas may mga fixed ratios, lot areas, frontage, open space percentages. So ang sagot dito malamang ay iyong standard pair, hindi iyong "pwede na siguro."
Pangatlo, kapag may naalala kang line na lot should not exceed 40%, tapos na agad.
Kasi kung lot = 40%, house = 60%.
Kaya kahit hindi mo kabisado ang buong paragraph ng law, puwede mong ma-eliminate ang 70-30, 75-25, at 80-20 dahil wala silang tumatama sa tested BP 220 memory point. (DHSUD)
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay akalain mong ang "mas mahalaga" ang house, so baka 70-30 o 80-20. Pero hindi ito subjective valuation question. Codal ratio memory item ito.
Philippine basis
BP 220 Revised IRR, economic and socialized housing standards. The lot component for a house-and-lot package is capped in a way that yields the tested 60%-40% ratio. (DHSUD)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag BP 220 at tinanong ang house-and-lot selling price ratio, tandaan mo agad: 60% house, 40% lot.
________________________________________

question: Prior to the submission of Building Permit, the owner and/or the professional designer shall secure the?
keyMemory: Kapag narinig mo ang phrase na "before building permit", unang papasok sa isip mo dapat: Locational / Zoning Clearance.
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"Prior to the submission of Building Permit, the owner and/or the professional designer shall secure the?"
Choices:
a. Locational / Zoning Clearance
b. Accessory Clearance
c. Amendatory Permit
d. Fire Safety Inspection Certificate
Correct answer: a. Locational / Zoning Clearance.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay Locational or Zoning Clearance.
Ito, napakagandang ALE question kasi ang clue mismo nasa phrase na "prior to the submission of Building Permit." Bago ka mag-building permit, ang unang chine-check ay kung allowed ba ang project sa location under the zoning ordinance at land use rules. Kaya Locational/Zoning Clearance ang logical at legal na nauuna. Multiple LGU and official guidance sources state that a valid locational clearance is required before a building permit is issued. (City Government of Lipa)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Kasi ang building permit process, bago pa pumasok sa detailed technical review, chine-check muna kung ang project mo ay conforming sa zoning at land use ordinance. Kaya nga sa page mo rin, sa "Processing of Building Permit," item 1 says the OBO verifies conformity with the land use zoning ordinance, and the note says prior to submission, the owner/professional designer shall secure the Locational / Zoning Clearance. Iyan ang tamang exam logic: site legality muna bago technical permit. Official local government guidance also says no building permit shall be issued without a valid locational clearance. (City Government of Lipa)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
b. Accessory Clearance — hindi ito ang standard primary pre-building-permit clearance na hinahanap dito. Mukhang legal-sounding, pero hindi ito ang classic prerequisite na tinutukoy ng tanong.
c. Amendatory Permit — mali agad ito kasi ang amendatory permit ay lumalabas kapag may changes or amendments sa nauna nang permit or approved plans. Hindi ito "prior to submission" requirement.
d. Fire Safety Inspection Certificate — ito ang magandang trap. Bakit? Kasi may fire safety involvement talaga sa permit process. Pero ang wording ng tanong is bago pa ang submission ng building permit, ano munang ise-secure? Sa standard sequencing, locational/zoning clearance muna. Ang fire safety documents and evaluations enter the process, but not as the primary zoning-related prerequisite being asked here. (City Government of Lipa)
Elimination method, step by step
Ganito ka mag-isip sa exam:
Una, pakinggan ang timeline word: "prior to the submission."
Timeline question ito. Hindi lang "required," kundi alin ang mauuna.
Pangalawa, isipin mo ang permit logic.
Bago mo pa pag-usapan ang structural, plumbing, electrical, or fire, dapat malinaw muna na pwede kang magtayo diyan under zoning.
Pangatlo, alisin mo ang Amendatory Permit kasi may existing permit muna bago magkaroon ng amendment.
So obvious wrong agad iyan.
Pang-apat, alisin mo ang Accessory Clearance kung walang strong Philippine code anchor.
Mukhang distractor lang.
Panglima, pag natira ang Locational/Zoning Clearance at FSIC, piliin mo ang mas basic at mas nauna sa process.
Ang mas nauna ay Locational/Zoning Clearance.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay pinaparamdam sa'yo na dahil building laws ito, baka dapat fire-related agad ang piliin. Pero tandaan mo: zoning comes before building permit evaluation.
Ang tanong ay hindi "ano ang related sa permit," kundi ano ang ise-secure prior to submission.
Philippine basis
Official LGU and planning/building guidelines consistently require a Locational or Zoning Clearance before issuance or processing of the building permit, because it establishes conformity with the zoning ordinance and land use controls. (City Government of Lipa)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag narinig mo ang phrase na "before building permit", unang papasok sa isip mo dapat: Locational / Zoning Clearance.
________________________________________

question: In case the building owner is not the owner of the lot he/she is building on, what must they provide to the Building Official in application to Building permit?
keyMemory: Kapag builder is not the lot owner, isipin mo agad: kailangan ng authority or permission from the lot owner. Sa choices na ito, ang best answer ay Lot Owner's Consent. Sige, tuloy tayo.
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"In case the building owner is not the owner of the lot he/she is building on, what must they provide to the Building Official in application to Building permit?"
Choices:
a. Deed of Sale
b. Lot Owner's Consent
c. Special Power of Attorney
d. Acknowledgement Note
Correct answer: b. Lot Owner's Consent.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay Lot Owner's Consent.
Ito medyo maganda kasi may konting real-world nuance. Sa actual permitting practice, kapag ang applicant ay hindi registered owner ng lot, hinihingan siya ng documents proving authority or right to build on the land. Sa official checklists, this often appears as the registered owner's written consent and/or SPA, plus other supporting documents like lease or deed of sale depending on the situation. Pero sa choices ng tanong na ito, ang best ALE answer ay Lot Owner's Consent.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Pansinin mo ang sitwasyon:
building owner ≠ lot owner.
Ibig sabihin, may separation between who will build and who owns the land. In that case, ang pinaka-basic at pinakadirect requirement ay permission from the lot owner. Iyan ang essence ng Lot Owner's Consent. Sa actual LGU checklists, makikita mo ito as the registered owner's written consent or affidavit of consent, sometimes together with other proofs depending on the exact legal relationship.
Bakit hindi deed of sale?
Ito ang pinakamagandang i-eliminate.
Kapag may Deed of Sale, ibig sabihin may transfer or intended transfer of ownership interest. Pero ang tanong mismo says the building owner is not the owner of the lot. So hindi automatic na deed of sale ang hinihingi. Puwede kasing hindi naman binebenta ang lupa — baka pinapayagan lang siyang magtayo, baka lease, baka authority lang. Kaya mas general at mas directly responsive ang Lot Owner's Consent kaysa Deed of Sale. Also, the NBCP IRR mentions deed of absolute sale or contract of lease as examples of documentary support when the applicant is not the registered owner, which shows deed of sale is only one possible scenario, not the universal answer. (philarchitecturenotes.weebly.com)
Bakit hindi Special Power of Attorney?
Importanteng distinction ito.
Ang SPA is authority to represent someone in transactions. Ibig sabihin, ginagamit ito kapag may representative na kikilos on behalf of the owner or applicant. Pero ang question ay hindi tungkol sa representative issue. Ang tanong ay tungkol sa lot ownership issue. So unless the specific problem is that someone else is filing on behalf of the owner, the more direct answer remains Lot Owner's Consent. In actual checklists, SPA may also appear, but usually as an alternative or additional authority document, not the clearest answer to the ownership-consent problem being tested here.
Bakit mali ang Acknowledgement Note?
Ito ang madaling i-throw away.
Hindi ito ang standard documentary term na ginagamit sa Philippine building permit requirements. Kapag may choice na parang legal pakinggan pero hindi standard permitting document, malaking chance na distractor lang iyon.
Elimination method, step by step
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Una, i-translate mo ang tanong sa simple terms:
"Hindi sa kaniya ang lupa. Ano ang kailangan niya para makapag-apply?"
Sagot: patunay na pinapayagan siya ng may-ari ng lupa.
Pangalawa, alisin agad ang Acknowledgement Note.
Hindi standard.
Pangatlo, pag-isipan ang Deed of Sale.
Masyado itong specific. Hindi lahat ng hindi lot owner ay bumibili ng lote. So hindi ito best general answer.
Pang-apat, pag-isipan ang SPA.
Authority to transact iyan, not necessarily authority to build on land you do not own.
Panglima, ang natitira at pinakadiretso sa issue ay Lot Owner's Consent.
Iyan ang reason bakit natitira ito sa choices.
Iyan din ang reason bakit natatanggal ang iba.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay pinaghahalo ang ownership proof, authority to represent, at permission to build.
Tatlong magkaibang bagay iyan.
Sa tanong na ito, ang pinaka-core issue ay permission from the landowner.
Kaya Lot Owner's Consent ang pinakatamang ALE answer.
Philippine basis
The NBCP IRR and actual OBO checklists require additional proof when the applicant is not the registered owner of the lot. In practice, this includes the registered owner's written consent and/or SPA, along with other supporting documents like lease or deed of sale when applicable. Given the options here, Lot Owner's Consent is the best fit.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag builder is not the lot owner, isipin mo agad: kailangan ng authority or permission from the lot owner. Sa choices na ito, ang best answer ay Lot Owner's Consent.
Sige, tuloy tayo.

question: Which among the following is considered as minor construction and may be exempted for application of Building Permit?

choices.text: Playhouse not more than 6 sqm for public use
Patio directly resting on the ground not more than 30 sqm
Garden pools for cultivation of plants not exceeding 600 sqm
Garden masonry walls other than party walls not exceeding 1.20 m in height

answer: D. Garden masonry walls other than party walls not exceeding 1.20 m in height
keyMemory: Kapag ang tanong ay tungkol sa minor construction exempt from building permit, hanapin mo ang choice na: • maliit • controlled ang dimension • hindi for public use • hindi exaggerated ang sukat • hindi major structure Sa choices na ito, ang malinaw na pasok diyan ay: Garden masonry walls other than party walls not exceeding 1.20 m in height. Sige, tuloy tayo sa Page 4. Dalawa ang visible questions dito, so sunod-sunod natin.
explanation:
Basahin muna natin ang full question:
"Which among the following is considered as minor construction and may be exempted for application of Building Permit?"
Choices:
a. Playhouse not more than 6 sqm for public use
b. Patio directly resting on the ground not more than 30 sqm
c. Garden pools for cultivation of plants not exceeding 600 sqm
d. Garden masonry walls other than party walls not exceeding 1.20 m in height
Correct answer: d. Garden masonry walls other than party walls not exceeding 1.20 m in height.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay letter d, garden masonry walls other than party walls not exceeding 1.20 meters in height.
Ngayon himayin natin nang maigi, kasi ito ang klaseng ALE question na kaya mong sagutin kahit hindi mo kabisado word-for-word ang code, basta marunong kang mag-eliminate.
Bakit ito ang tamang sagot?
Ang main clue dito ay: "minor construction" at "may be exempted for application of Building Permit."
Sa PD 1096 o National Building Code, may mga minor constructions na hindi na kailangan ng full building permit dahil sobrang liit, simple, at low-risk lang ng impact nila. Isa sa mga classic examples niyan ay garden masonry walls other than party walls, basta hindi lalampas sa 1.20 meters ang taas.
So ang iniisip mo rito:
Hindi ito major structure.
Hindi ito public-use occupancy issue.
Hindi ito malaking built area.
Hindi ito something na obvious na may bigger safety implication.
Kaya letter d ang pasok na pasok sa definition ng minor construction.
________________________________________
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Playhouse not more than 6 sqm for public use
Ito, sobrang importanteng trap ito.
Mapapansin mo, may phrase na "not more than 6 sqm", na mukhang tama kasi oo, may mga small playhouses or similar very small accessory structures na puwedeng ma-consider under minor construction.
Pero ang killer word dito ay: "for public use."
Ayan ang trap.
Kapag for public use, tumataas ang level of regulation, safety concern, at occupancy concern. Hindi na siya basta simpleng minor exempt structure lang. Sa board exam, kapag may choice na mukhang tama sa simula pero may isang salitang sumisira sa logic, iyon ang trap answer.
So ang iniisip mo dapat:
"Sandali, puwedeng maliit nga, pero bakit public use? Delikado ito. Mukhang hindi ito exempt."
Kaya tanggal agad si a.
________________________________________
b. Patio directly resting on the ground not more than 30 sqm
Ito rin, classic trap.
Ang idea ng patio resting on the ground, oo, puwedeng pumasok sa minor work territory depende sa size at form. Pero ang problem dito ay ang 30 sqm. Masyado itong malaki para sa typical exempt minor construction item na gusto ng ALE.
Sa exam, kapag may structure or improvement na lumalaki ang area, nagiging less believable na exempt siya.
Kaya kahit hindi mo kabisado exact number, puwede mong isipin:
"Minor construction ba talaga ang 30 sqm patio? Medyo malaki na iyan."
So kahit mukhang technical ang wording, ang number ang red flag.
Kaya malabong ito ang best answer.
________________________________________
c. Garden pools for cultivation of plants not exceeding 600 sqm
Ito naman, halos obvious na distractor na.
600 sqm?
Napakalaki niyan.
Kapag board exam at may choice na sobrang exaggerated ang sukat, very often iyon ang madaling tanggalin. Kasi ang tanong ay minor construction, tapos bibigyan ka ng isang option na halos parang isang malaking site feature na.
Ito ang tawag kong "exaggerated choice trap."
Mukhang legal pakinggan, pero binaril ng sobrang laking sukat.
Kaya automatic, tanggal agad.
________________________________________
Elimination method, step by step
Ito ang gusto kong matutunan mo talaga, kasi ito ang gagamitin mo sa actual ALE.
Step 1: Hanapin ang category
Ang tanong ay tungkol sa minor construction exempt from building permit.
So ang hinahanap mo ay:
• maliit
• low-risk
• simple
• non-public
• non-major structural impact
Iyan ang mindset.
________________________________________
Step 2: Hanapin ang trap words
Tingnan natin isa-isa:
• a. for public use → trap word
• b. 30 sqm → suspiciously large
• c. 600 sqm → exaggerated, halos impossible as "minor"
• d. not exceeding 1.20 m height → mukhang controlled, limited, small-scale
Kita mo?
Hindi mo pa kailangan kabisado ang buong code.
Ginagamit mo ang logic ng minor = limited.
________________________________________
Step 3: Tanggalin ang obviously exaggerated
Unang tatanggalin mo: c.
Kasi 600 sqm is way too big.
________________________________________
Step 4: Tanggalin ang may occupancy/public-use issue
Sunod tatanggalin mo: a.
Kasi "public use" agad.
Kapag public use, hindi mo basta-basta ie-exempt iyan.
________________________________________
Step 5: Compare the last two
Natira: b at d.
Ngayon, alin ang mas mukhang classic exempt minor construction item?
• patio at 30 sqm
• masonry wall not exceeding 1.20 m
Mas believable at mas code-like ang low garden wall up to 1.20 m.
Kaya d ang matitira.
________________________________________
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot
Habang binabasa mo ang choices, sabihin mo sa isip mo:
"Minor construction ito. So dapat maliit, simple, hindi public, hindi sobrang laki, hindi high-risk."
Tapos tingnan mo ang options:
• public use? alisin
• sobrang laking area? alisin
• exaggerated dimension? alisin
• maliit, limited-height garden wall? ito ang natitira
Ayan. Iyan ang board exam elimination.
________________________________________
Common trap sa ALE
Ang common trap dito ay dalawang bagay:
Una, pinapasilaw ka sa technical wording.
Minsan akala mo pag mahaba at komplikado ang choice, iyon ang tama. Hindi lagi.
Pangalawa, nilalagyan nila ng isang maling salita ang otherwise familiar item.
Example: "playhouse not more than 6 sqm" sounds okay… pero may "for public use."
Ayan ang sumisira.
Kaya sa ALE, huwag ka lang tumingin sa first half ng choice.
Basahin mo hanggang dulo.
________________________________________
Philippine basis
Ang basis nito ay PD 1096 / National Building Code of the Philippines, under Exemptions from Building Permits, particularly minor constructions. Ang garden wall na mababa at non-party wall is the kind of limited work that may be exempt, habang iyong may public-use implication or obviously oversized improvements ay hindi na papasok bilang minor exempt work.
________________________________________
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang tanong ay tungkol sa minor construction exempt from building permit, hanapin mo ang choice na:
• maliit
• controlled ang dimension
• hindi for public use
• hindi exaggerated ang sukat
• hindi major structure
Sa choices na ito, ang malinaw na pasok diyan ay:
Garden masonry walls other than party walls not exceeding 1.20 m in height.
Sige, tuloy tayo sa Page 4. Dalawa ang visible questions dito, so sunod-sunod natin.

question: When is the expiration of a building permit if construction has not been started?
keyMemory: 1 year kapag hindi pa nasisimulan. 120 days kapag nasimulan na pero natigil o na-abandon.
explanation:
Basahin muna natin ang full question:
"When is the expiration of a building permit if construction has not been started?"
Choices:
a. After 120 days
b. After 6 months
c. After 1 year
d. After 2 years
Correct answer: c. After 1 year.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay after 1 year.
Ito, very classic ALE item ito. At gusto kong malinaw sa utak mo ang distinction dito, kasi madalas pinaghahalo ng examinees ang 1 year at 120 days.
Sa PD 1096, the building permit becomes null and void if the work is not commenced within one year from the date of the permit. Iba pa iyon sa rule na 120 days, na applicable kapag nasimulan na ang trabaho pero na-suspend o na-abandon pagkatapos magsimula. (iibh.org)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang pinaka-keyword sa tanong ay: "if construction has not been started."
Iyan ang clue.
Hindi niya sinabing naumpisahan na tapos tumigil.
Ang sabi niya, hindi pa nasisimulan.
So ang tamang rule na aangkop diyan ay:
commence within one year from date of issue.
Kapag hindi nasimulan within one year, expired na ang permit. (iibh.org)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. After 120 days
Ito ang pinaka-common trap.
Bakit? Kasi totoo rin ang 120 days, pero para iyon sa suspension or abandonment after commencement. Ibig sabihin, nagsimula na ang construction, tapos napatigil o pinabayaan nang 120 days. Hindi iyan ang sitwasyon sa tanong. (iibh.org)
b. After 6 months
Walang standard PD 1096 rule dito na 6 months ang expiration if not started. Kaya filler distractor lang ito.
d. After 2 years
Mukhang plausible kasi parang "mahaba ang validity," pero hindi ito ang codal rule. Sa NBCP, 1 year ang rule for non-commencement. (iibh.org)
Elimination method, step by step
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Step 1: Hanapin ang timeline condition.
Ang phrase na "has not been started" means non-commencement.
Step 2: Ihiwalay sa isip mo ang dalawang timeline rules:
• 1 year = not commenced
• 120 days = suspended/abandoned after starting
Ito ang reason bakit madalas nalilito ang examinee. Parehong time period, pero magkaibang sitwasyon. (iibh.org)
Step 3: Tanggalin agad ang 120 days kapag hindi pa nasisimulan ang project.
Kasi hindi pa applicable ang suspension or abandonment kung wala pang nasisimulan.
Step 4: Sa natirang 6 months, 1 year, 2 years, piliin ang codal rule.
That is 1 year.
Ito ang reason bakit natitira ito sa choices
Kasi ang tanong ay tungkol sa expiration due to non-start, and the code text directly says not commenced within one year. (iibh.org)
Common trap sa board exam
Ang common trap dito ay:
pag nakita mo ang word na expiration, biglang papasok sa isip mo ang 120 days kasi kabisado mo iyon. Pero dapat tanungin mo ang sarili mo:
"Nagsimula na ba ang construction sa problem?"
Kung hindi pa, then hindi 120 days.
One year ang sagot.
Philippine basis
PD 1096, Section 305 – Validity of Building Permits: a building permit expires if the work is not commenced within one year from the date of the permit, or if work already commenced is suspended or abandoned for 120 days. (iibh.org)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
1 year kapag hindi pa nasisimulan.
120 days kapag nasimulan na pero natigil o na-abandon.
________________________________________

question: After construction has been completed, what should be secured prior using the building?
keyMemory: Kapag ang tanong ay "bago gamitin o i-occupy ang building", automatic mong isipin: Certificate of Occupancy. Sige, Page 5 tayo. May 5 visible questions dito, so tuloy-tuloy natin from
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"After construction has been completed, what should be secured prior using the building?"
Choices:
a. FSIC
b. Building Permit
c. Locational Clearance
d. Certificate of Occupancy
Correct answer: d. Certificate of Occupancy.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay Certificate of Occupancy.
Ito rin, sobrang importanteng ALE staple ito. Sa PD 1096, no building or structure shall be used or occupied until the Building Official has issued a Certificate of Occupancy. Iyan mismo ang legal logic. Kaya after completion, before actual use, ang hahanapin mo ay Certificate of Occupancy. (iibh.org)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Pansinin mo ang tanong:
"After construction has been completed"
at
"prior using the building"
Ibig sabihin, tapos na ang construction phase. So tapos na rin ang role ng building permit as permission to construct. Ang kailangan mo na ngayon ay authorization to use/occupy the building.
At iyon ay exactly what the Certificate of Occupancy is for. The Code says a building cannot be used or occupied until that certificate is issued. (iibh.org)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. FSIC
Ito ang best trap sa item na ito.
Bakit? Kasi totoo naman na fire safety inspection and clearances matter, and in practice the FSIC is commonly required before the occupancy permit process is completed. Pero ang tanong ay: ano ang dapat i-secure prior using the building?
Ang direct legal answer under PD 1096 is still Certificate of Occupancy. Ang FSIC is important, yes, but it is not the final use authorization named by the Building Code in this question. (iibh.org)
b. Building Permit
Mali ito kasi ang building permit ay para makapagtayo.
Once tapos na ang construction, hindi ito ang hinahanap prior to use. Nauna na ito sa process.
c. Locational Clearance
Mas mali ito sa stage logic.
Locational or zoning clearance comes before building permit processing, not after completion and before use.
Elimination method, step by step
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Step 1: Tingnan ang project stage.
Ang tanong ay nasa post-construction stage na.
Step 2: Alisin ang documents na pang-early stage.
• Locational Clearance = pre-building permit
• Building Permit = pre-construction
Tanggal agad iyan dahil tapos na ang building.
Step 3: Compare the last two: FSIC vs Certificate of Occupancy.
Dito ka madadale kung hindi malinaw ang hierarchy.
Ang tanong ay hindi "anong fire-related document ang kailangan?"
Ang tanong ay ano ang dapat i-secure bago gamitin ang building?
The final use/occupancy authorization is Certificate of Occupancy. (iibh.org)
Ito ang reason bakit natitira ito sa choices
Kasi ang certificate mismo ang legal permission to occupy or use the completed building. FSIC supports fire safety compliance, pero the occupancy/use authorization in PD 1096 is the Certificate of Occupancy. (iibh.org)
Common trap sa board exam
Ang common trap dito ay FSIC vs Certificate of Occupancy.
Tandaan mo ito:
FSIC is fire-safety related compliance.
Certificate of Occupancy is the final authority to use or occupy the building.
So kapag board exam wording is "prior using the building", ang mas direct at codal answer ay Certificate of Occupancy. (iibh.org)
Philippine basis
PD 1096, Section 309 – Certificate of Occupancy says no building or structure shall be used or occupied until the Building Official issues the certificate. Local OBO guidance also states that a certificate of occupancy or use must be secured prior to occupancy or use of any building or structure. (iibh.org)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang tanong ay "bago gamitin o i-occupy ang building", automatic mong isipin: Certificate of Occupancy.
Sige, Page 5 tayo. May 5 visible questions dito, so tuloy-tuloy natin from Question 11.
________________________________________

question: As per NBCP, what type of construction consists of a combination of masonry and wood and requiring that building should be one-hour fire-resistive throughout?
keyMemory: Type III = masonry and wood + one-hour fire-resistive throughout.
explanation:
Basahin muna natin ang full question:
"As per NBCP, what type of construction consists of a combination of masonry and wood and requiring that building should be one-hour fire-resistive throughout?"
Choices:
a. Type I and III
b. Type III and IV
c. Type I
d. Type III
Correct answer: d. Type III.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay Type III.
Ito, definition question ito, pero may trap sa choices. Sa NBCP, Type III ang described as masonry and wood construction, and the building is required to be one-hour fire-resistive throughout; exterior walls are incombustible fire-resistive construction. Iyan mismo ang keyword match ng tanong. (Scribd)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Pansinin mo ang dalawang strongest clues:
"combination of masonry and wood" at "one-hour fire-resistive throughout."
Hindi mo na kailangan hulaan iyan. Parehong tumatama sa Type III. Kapag board exam may direct phrase match sa code definition, iyon ang pinaka-safe at pinaka-malinaw na sagot. (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Type I and III — trap ito. Bakit? Kasi may Type III sa choice, so parang tempting piliin. Pero compound answer ito. Kapag may isang maling kasama, mali na ang buong choice. Type I is wood construction, not the specific masonry-and-wood one-hour fire-resistive description na hinahanap ng tanong. (Philippine Architecture 101)
b. Type III and IV — same trap. Oo, may Type III ulit, pero nadagdagan ng Type IV, which refers to steel, iron, concrete, or masonry construction with incombustible fire-resistive walls, ceilings, and permanent partitions. Hindi iyan ang "masonry and wood" type. (Philippine Architecture 101)
c. Type I — mali kasi Type I is wood construction, hindi masonry and wood. So kahit may wood, kulang ang clue match. (Philippine Architecture 101)
Elimination method, step by step
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Una, hanapin mo ang exact phrase match.
Kapag may wording na parang galing mismo sa code, huwag ka nang mag-overthink.
Pangalawa, tanggalin mo ang mga compound options kung alam mong isang type lang ang exact definition.
Sa ALE, madalas nilalagyan ka nila ng "Type I and III" para madale ka dahil nakita mo lang ang Type III.
Pangatlo, compare mo ang natira:
Type I versus Type III.
Alin ang may masonry and wood plus one-hour fire resistive throughout?
Siyempre Type III.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang common trap dito ay half-right option.
Kapag may choice na may kasamang tamang term pero may idinagdag na maling term, huwag mo piliin.
Philippine basis
NBCP classification of types of construction: Type III is masonry and wood construction and must be one-hour fire-resistive throughout. (Scribd)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Type III = masonry and wood + one-hour fire-resistive throughout.
________________________________________

question: What is the LIVE LOAD requirement for Group A dwelling at second floor level?
keyMemory: Group A dwelling: 1st floor 200, 2nd floor 150, roof 120 wind load.
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"What is the LIVE LOAD requirement for Group A dwelling at second floor level?"
Choices:
a. 200 kg/sqm
b. 150 kg/sqm
c. 120 kg/sqm
d. 100 kg/sqm
Correct answer: b. 150 kg/sqm.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay 150 kilograms per square meter.
Sa Group A dwellings under PD 1096 references, the first floor live load is at least 200 kg/sqm, and the second floor is at least 150 kg/sqm. Yung 120 kg/sqm na nakikita mo sa page ay para sa roof wind load, hindi para sa second-floor live load. Iyan ang pinaka-importanteng distinction dito. (Scribd)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Keyword dito: "second floor level."
Hindi first floor. Hindi roof. Hindi general live load lang.
Pag second floor ng Group A dwelling, ang direct figure ay 150 kg/sqm. (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. 200 kg/sqm — mali kasi ito ang ground/first floor requirement, hindi second floor. (Scribd)
c. 120 kg/sqm — ito ang classic trap. Mukha siyang familiar kasi nasa same set of figures sa page, pero para iyan sa roof wind load, hindi second-floor live load. (Scribd)
d. 100 kg/sqm — walang basis dito sa Group A second-floor requirement sa tanong.
Elimination method, step by step
Ganito ka mag-isip:
Una, tandaan mo ang tatlong figures na magkakatabi:
200, 150, 120.
Pangalawa, i-assign mo sila nang tama:
200 = first floor
150 = second floor
120 = roof wind load (Scribd)
Pangatlo, dahil second floor ang tanong, tanggal agad ang 200 at 120.
Pag ganyan ang exam, hindi lang memory ang sinusukat — sinusukat din kung nalilito ka sa magkakapit na numbers.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay number confusion.
Kapag magkakalapit ang values, madalas doon nagkakamali ang examinee, hindi dahil hindi alam, kundi dahil napagpalit.
Philippine basis
Group A dwelling minimum live loads under PD 1096 references: first floor 200 kg/sqm, second floor 150 kg/sqm, roof wind load 120 kg/sqm. (Scribd)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Group A dwelling: 1st floor 200, 2nd floor 150, roof 120 wind load.
________________________________________

question: For a dwelling with a load of less than 10 occupants, what is the minimum stair width required?
keyMemory: Less than 10 occupants = 750 mm stair width.
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"For a dwelling with a load of less than 10 occupants, what is the minimum stair width required?"
Choices:
a. 700 mm
b. 750 mm
c. 900 mm
d. 1100 mm
Correct answer: b. 750 mm.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay 750 millimeters.
Sa PD 1096 stair requirements, private stairways serving an occupant load of less than 10 may be 750 mm wide. Samantalang 900 mm applies to stairways serving an occupant load of 50 or less, at 1.10 m applies to more than 50 occupants. (DPWH)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang clue ay sobrang specific: "less than 10 occupants."
Ibig sabihin, hindi general stair rule ang hanap. Private-dwelling stair rule ito. At doon, the minimum width may be 750 mm. (DPWH)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. 700 mm — mukhang realistic, pero wala itong basis sa specific stair rule dito.
c. 900 mm — ito ang trap. Totoo ito sa ibang stair situation, specifically for stairways serving an occupant load of 50 or less. Pero dahil mas specific ang tanong — less than 10 occupants — mas maliit at mas direct ang special rule na 750 mm. (DPWH)
d. 1100 mm — ito naman ang requirement for stairways serving more than 50 occupants, kaya sobrang laki para sa tanong. (DPWH)
Elimination method, step by step
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat:
Una, hanapin kung may special case.
Dito meron: less than 10 occupants.
Pangalawa, kapag may special case, huwag ka kukuha ng mas general rule.
General rules are:
900 mm for 50 or less
1100 mm for more than 50
Pero may mas special na exception para sa private stairways of less than 10 occupants: 750 mm. (DPWH)
Pangatlo, tanggalin ang 1100, then 900, then 700.
Matitira ang 750 mm.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay general rule versus special rule.
Kapag may mas specific wording ang question, doon ka dapat sumunod.
Philippine basis
PD 1096 stair width rules: private stairways serving less than 10 occupants may be 750 mm; 50 or less may be 900 mm; more than 50 shall not be less than 1.10 m. (DPWH)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Less than 10 occupants = 750 mm stair width.
________________________________________

question: Group A dwelling shall occupy not more than ___% (PSO) of an inside lot?
keyMemory: Group A dwelling PSO: 90% corner lot, 80% inside lot.
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"Group A dwelling shall occupy not more than ___% (PSO) of an inside lot?"
Choices:
a. 50%
b. 60%
c. 70%
d. 80%
Correct answer: d. 80%.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay 80 percent.
For Group A dwellings, the dwelling shall occupy not more than 90% of a corner lot and 80% of an inside lot. So kapag inside lot ang tinanong, the answer is 80% PSO. (architectureboard.com.ph)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Keyword dito: "inside lot."
Huwag mong mapagpalit sa corner lot.
Sa Group A dwelling lot occupancy rule, mas mataas ang allowable occupancy sa corner lot, pero sa inside lot, 80% ang cap. (architectureboard.com.ph)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
50%, 60%, 70% — puwede silang mukhang believable, pero hindi sila ang codal figure sa tanong.
Ang tested pair dito ay:
90% corner lot
80% inside lot (architectureboard.com.ph)
Elimination method, step by step
Ganito ang board strategy:
Una, tandaan mo na madalas pair ang lumalabas sa memory item na ito.
Hindi mo dapat minememorya nang pira-piraso lang.
Pangalawa, kabisaduhin mo ang pair:
corner lot = 90
inside lot = 80 (www.mysubicbay.com.ph)
Pangatlo, kapag inside lot ang word na nakita mo, automatic mong tanggalin ang lower random numbers kung wala kang basis sa code.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay simpleng number swap.
Minsan alam mo ang 90 at 80, pero napagpapalit mo kung alin ang para sa corner at alin ang para sa inside lot.
Philippine basis
PD 1096 Group A dwelling lot occupancy provisions: 90% of a corner lot, 80% of an inside lot. (architectureboard.com.ph)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Group A dwelling PSO: 90% corner lot, 80% inside lot.
________________________________________

question: A dwelling on one lot containing separate living units for 3 or more families, usually provided with common access, services, and use of land?
keyMemory: Kapag narinig mo ang "3 or more families on one lot, with common access/services/use of land," isipin mo agad: Multi-family dwelling. Sige, tuloy tayo sa Page 6. May 3 visible questions dito, so continue tayo sa numbering.
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"A dwelling on one lot containing separate living units for 3 or more families, usually provided with common access, services, and use of land?"
Choices:
a. Cluster Housing
b. Multi-family dwelling
c. Rowhouse
d. Planned Unit Dev't
Correct answer: b. Multi-family dwelling.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay Multi-family dwelling.
Ito ay halos direct definition question. The phrase "a dwelling on one lot containing separate living units for 3 or more families, usually provided with common access, services, and use of land" matches the standard definition of multi-family dwelling. (Studocu)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Pansinin mo ang exact clues:
one lot, separate living units, 3 or more families, common access, services, and use of land.
Iyan ang language ng multi-family dwelling. Hindi ito attached-housing pattern lang. Hindi rin ito broader site-planning concept. Definition talaga ito ng occupancy/housing type. (Studocu)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Cluster Housing — malapit ito sa housing development language, pero hindi ito ang exact definition na binibigay sa tanong.
c. Rowhouse — mali ito kasi rowhouse units are typically thought of as attached units with independent access. Iyan din ang clue na nakasulat sa page mo sa baba. So kung sinabi ng tanong na common access, services, and use of land, hindi rowhouse ang best answer. (creba.ph)
d. Planned Unit Dev't — sobrang broad ito. Development concept ito, hindi direct dwelling-type definition ng item.
Elimination method, step by step
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Una, tanungin mo:
Definition ba ito ng building type o site-planning concept?
Mukhang building type.
Pangalawa, hanapin ang defining words:
3 or more families
separate living units
common access/services/use of land
Pangatlo, alisin ang planned unit development kasi mas malawak iyan.
Alisin ang cluster housing kung hindi exact definition match.
Alisin ang rowhouse dahil independent access ang usual clue diyan.
Matitira ang multi-family dwelling. (Studocu)
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay magkakalapit na housing terms.
Board exam loves that. Hindi ka naman nililinlang sa totally alien words — nililinlang ka sa magkakamukhang tama.
Philippine basis
The definition matching 3 or more families in separate living units on one lot with common access, services, and use of land is multi-family dwelling. Rowhouses, by contrast, are associated with independent access per unit. (Studocu)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag narinig mo ang "3 or more families on one lot, with common access/services/use of land," isipin mo agad: Multi-family dwelling.
Sige, tuloy tayo sa Page 6. May 3 visible questions dito, so continue tayo sa numbering.
________________________________________

question: It is a zone wherein only non-combustible materials are allowed to be used as construction material?
keyMemory: Kapag may phrase na "only non-combustible materials allowed," ang sagot mo agad ay Highly-Fire Restrictive Zone.
explanation:
Basahin muna natin ang full question:
"It is a zone wherein only non-combustible materials are allowed to be used as construction material?"
Choices:
a. Non-Fire Restrictive Zone
b. Fire Resistive Zone
c. Moderate Fire Resistive Zone
d. Highly-Fire Restrictive Zone
Correct answer: d. Highly-Fire Restrictive Zone.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay Highly-Fire Restrictive Zone.
Ito, direct definition question ito. Sa PD 1096, ang Highly Fire Restrictive Zone ang zone kung saan highly fire-resistive or non-combustible buildings at construction assemblies ang pinapayagan, at only Types IV and V constructions are permitted there. Ibig sabihin, ito ang pinakamahigpit sa choices pagdating sa paggamit ng non-combustible materials. (VIZCODE)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang pinaka-clue dito ay ang phrase na "only non-combustible materials are allowed."
Kapag nakita mo ang salitang only at non-combustible, isipin mo agad na ito na ang pinakamahigpit na fire zone classification. Hindi puwedeng ordinary wood construction dito. Kaya hindi ito non-fire restrictive, at hindi rin simpleng fire restrictive lang. Dapat doon ka sa highest restriction level, which is Highly-Fire Restrictive Zone. (VIZCODE)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Non-Fire Restrictive Zone — ito ang kabaligtaran halos. Dito mas maluwag, at puwedeng walang fire-resistivity measures in the way required in more restrictive zones. Kaya hindi ito puwedeng sagot kapag sinabi ng tanong na only non-combustible. (architectureboard.com.ph)
b. Fire Resistive Zone — may fire-resistivity measures ito, pero hindi ibig sabihin na only non-combustible materials ang pwede. Mas mababa pa rin ang restriction nito kaysa highly fire restrictive zone. (VIZCODE)
c. Moderate Fire Resistive Zone — ito ang malinaw na distractor. Hindi ito ang standard term na nakikita mo sa PD 1096 fire zone classification sa tanong na ito. Kapag may option na parang legal-sounding pero hindi siya yung codal wording na kabisado mo, mag-ingat ka na. (VIZCODE)
Elimination method, step by step
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Step 1: Hanapin ang absolute word.
Dito ang absolute word ay "only."
Kapag may "only," hanap mo ang most restrictive choice.
Step 2: Tanggalin agad ang Non-Fire Restrictive Zone.
Kasi hindi puwedeng "only non-combustible" ang ibig sabihin ng non-fire restrictive.
Step 3: Tanggalin ang Moderate Fire Resistive Zone kung hindi mo siya kilala bilang exact codal label.
Board exam trap iyan: mukhang tama pakinggan pero hindi exact term.
Step 4: Compare Fire Resistive Zone vs Highly-Fire Restrictive Zone.
Alin ang mas mahigpit? Siyempre Highly-Fire Restrictive Zone.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay iniisip ng examinee na pareho lang ang fire resistive at highly fire restrictive. Hindi.
Kapag may clue na only non-combustible, huwag ka sa gitnang level. Doon ka sa pinakamataas na restriction.
Philippine basis
PD 1096 classifies Highly Fire Restrictive Zones as areas where only highly fire-resistive or non-combustible construction is allowed, with only Types IV and V permitted. (VIZCODE)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag may phrase na "only non-combustible materials allowed," ang sagot mo agad ay Highly-Fire Restrictive Zone.

question: Electrical fire should be suppressed by?
keyMemory: Kapag electrical fire, automatic alisin ang water at foam. Sa ALE single-answer logic, tandaan mo: CO2 extinguisher. Sige, Page 7 tayo. Magsisimula ako sa pinaka-itaas na visible question sa page, tapos tuloy-tuloy tayo.
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"Electrical fire should be suppressed by?"
Choices:
a. Water Fire Extinguisher
b. Foam Fire Extinguisher
c. CO2 Fire Extinguisher
d. Dry Powder Fire Extinguisher
Correct answer: c. CO2 Fire Extinguisher.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay CO2 Fire Extinguisher.
Dito kailangan medyo maingat tayo. Under fire classifications, electrical fires are Class C. The Fire Code says Class C hazards should use extinguishers specifically listed for Class C hazards, and BFP guidance commonly mentions carbon dioxide or dry chemical for electrical fires. Pero sa ganitong ALE-style single-answer question, the best and most expected answer is CO2 Fire Extinguisher, kasi ito ang classic choice for energized electrical equipment: non-conductive, at walang residue na puwedeng makasira sa equipment. (Internet Archive)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang clue dito ay "electrical fire."
So automatic, isipin mo Class C. Kapag energized electrical equipment ang pinag-uusapan, hindi puwedeng water. Hindi rin ideal ang foam. Between CO2 and dry powder, parehong puwedeng lumabas in broader fire-safety guidance for Class C, pero kapag isang sagot lang ang ALE reviewer wants, CO2 ang mas direct at mas classic answer for electrical fires. (Facebook)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Water Fire Extinguisher — mali agad. Water is not suitable where electricity is involved. Delikado ito because of conductivity concerns. (VIZCODE)
b. Foam Fire Extinguisher — hindi rin ito ang best answer for energized electrical fires. Even when foam is useful in some fire classes, it is not the standard go-to answer for live electrical equipment. (VIZCODE)
d. Dry Powder Fire Extinguisher — ito ang pinakamagandang trap. Bakit? Kasi puwede rin itong Class C-listed in many contexts. So practical fire-safety guidance may mention dry chemical or dry powder as acceptable for Class C. Pero sa reviewer-style one-best-answer format, CO2 ang mas expected answer because it is strongly associated with electrical fires and leaves no residue. (Internet Archive)
Elimination method, step by step
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Step 1: Electrical fire = Class C.
Step 2: Tanggalin agad ang water.
Ito ang pinaka-obvious na mali.
Step 3: Tanggalin ang foam.
Hindi ito ang classic ALE answer for energized electrical fires.
Step 4: Pag natira ang CO2 at Dry Powder, isipin mo ang board-exam logic:
Ano ang mas standard, mas classic, mas directly associated sa electrical equipment?
CO2.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay dalawang mukhang tama: CO2 at dry powder.
At totoo naman, in broader fire-safety practice, both may be listed for Class C depending on the extinguisher. Pero kapag ganitong reviewer item na single best answer, piliin mo ang CO2.
Philippine basis
RA 9514/its IRR classifies electrical fires as Class C and says extinguishers must be types specifically listed for Class C hazards. BFP public guidance commonly identifies carbon dioxide or dry chemical for electrical fires; in one-best-answer ALE format, CO2 is the standard pick. (Internet Archive)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag electrical fire, automatic alisin ang water at foam.
Sa ALE single-answer logic, tandaan mo: CO2 extinguisher.
Sige, Page 7 tayo. Magsisimula ako sa pinaka-itaas na visible question sa page, tapos tuloy-tuloy tayo.

explanation:
Basahin muna natin ang full question:
"Width of a dropped curb?"
Choices:
a. 800 mm
b. 900 mm
c. 1100 mm
d. 1200 mm
Correct answer: b. 900 mm.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay 900 mm.
Ito, accessibility item ito under BP 344 / DPWH accessibility standards. Ang key rule dito: ang dropped curb at pedestrian crossings should have a width corresponding to the width of the crossing if the sidewalk is less than 3.50 m wide; otherwise, the minimum width is 0.90 m. Kaya sa multiple choice na ito, ang tamang minimum answer ay 900 mm. (LegalDex AI)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Kasi ang tanong ay humihingi ng width ng dropped curb sa reviewer format. Sa codal standard, ang minimum na binibigay ay 0.90 m. Sa choices, iyon ay 900 mm. (LegalDex AI)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
800 mm — kulang sa minimum.
1100 mm at 1200 mm — puwedeng mukhang "mas safe," pero hindi iyan ang tested minimum code value na hinahanap ng tanong. Sa board exam, hindi porke mas malaki ay mas tama. Ang hinahanap ay exact minimum requirement. (LegalDex AI)
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
• Accessibility law ito, so kadalasan ang tanong ay minimum clear dimension
• Hanapin mo ang number na pinaka-kilala sa BP 344 curb details
• Kapag nakita mo ang 0.90 m, iyon ang classic answer
So kahit nagdadalawang-isip ka sa 900 at 1200, tandaan mo: code minimum ang madalas tinatanong, hindi recommended larger size.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay pinapapili ka ng mas malaking sukat para magmukhang mas "accessible." Pero ALE logic is: minimum code requirement ang tandaan mo.
Philippine basis
DPWH Department Order No. 037-09, enforcing BP 344 along roads, states that dropped curbs at pedestrian crossings have a width corresponding to the crossing, otherwise the minimum width is 0.90 m. (LegalDex AI)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Dropped curb minimum width = 900 mm.
Q19 — Width of a dropped curb / dropped sidewalk
Correct 2024 answer: Hindi na fixed na 900 mm.
Sa 2024 Revised IRR of BP 344, ang rule ay: the width of the dropped sidewalk at crossings should correspond to the width of the crossing. Sa local roads, local ordinances can govern; sa national roads, may minimum crossing-related width requirements. So kung old reviewer choices lang ang ibinigay at puro fixed numbers, walang eksaktong old choice na tumatama sa current 2024 wording.
Bakit ito ang tama:
Dating reviewer style kasi, may isang fixed dimension lang na minememorize. Pero sa 2024 version, mas context-based na siya: nakadepende sa lapad ng crossing, hindi sa isang universal number lang. Kaya kung strict 2024 ang basis, hindi ko na ituturo ang "900 mm" as the default answer.
Paano ko siya ginagawa sa question:
Kapag ang tanong ay tungkol sa dropped curb sa crossing, una kong hinahanap kung old reviewer item ba ito o current code item.
Kung current 2024, ang iniisip ko ay:
"Fixed number ba ito?"
Kapag nakita ko sa code na crossing-width based siya, ibig sabihin obsolete ang old fixed-answer style.
So ang tamang exam habit mo rito ay: i-flag ang item as outdated kapag old choices ang gamit.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa 2024 BP 344, dropped sidewalk width = width of crossing, not a fixed 900 mm.

Choices:
a. Fire Block
b. Fire Wall
c. Retaining Wall
d. End Wall
Correct answer: b. Fire Wall.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay Fire Wall.
Ito, direct definition ito. The PD 957 IRR defines firewall as a wall that subdivides a building so as to resist the spread of fire, extending continuously through the building in the manner prescribed. So kung ang tanong ay simpleng "wall designed to prevent the spread of fire," the answer is Fire Wall. (DHSUD)
Bakit ito ang tama? Kasi Retaining wall is for earth or lateral soil support. End wall is just positional, nasa dulo ng building. Fire block is a different, narrower term in housing contexts; hindi iyan ang general code term na hinahanap kapag spread of fire ang usapan. The standard, most direct answer is Fire Wall. (DHSUD)
Elimination method: kapag may phrase na prevent the spread of fire, huwag kang padadala sa word na "block." Ang mas codal at mas universal term ay Fire Wall. (DHSUD)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Wall to prevent spread of fire = Fire Wall.

b. Height of lavatory is from the waist
c. Grab Bar
d. All of the above

Ito, gusto kong maging honest sa'yo: reviewer-style question ito more than a clean verbatim codal line. Hindi ito iyong exact wording na madali mong makikita sa isang section heading ng BP 344. Pero sa ALE reviewer logic, ang intended answer talaga dito ay All of the above, kasi lahat ng choices ay tumutukoy sa accessibility and age-friendly design concerns — overall size/location/design, support features like grab bars, at ergonomic fixture placement. (DPWH)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Kapag ang tanong ay general conditions of design for the aged, hindi lang isang element ang tinitingnan.
Hindi lang "may grab bar ba?"
Hindi lang "tama ba ang taas ng lavatory?"
Hindi lang "okay ba ang layout?"
Ang tamang mindset dito ay holistic design.
So ang iniisip mo dapat ay:
• appropriate size para kumilos nang maayos
• tamang location ng fixtures at access routes
• maayos na design
• support features like grab bars
• fixtures at heights na mas madaling gamitin ng elderly users

Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. size, location, design — tama ito, pero kulang. General considerations lang ito. Hindi pa nito nasasakop ang support detail tulad ng grab bars.
b. Height of lavatory is from the waist — may ergonomic thought ito, pero masyado itong makitid para maging buong sagot sa "general conditions of design."

Step 1: Basahin ang scope ng tanong.
Sabi niya: general conditions of design.
Broad ang tanong.
Step 2: Tingnan kung may broad summary choice.
Meron: All of the above.
Step 3: I-check kung obviously mali ba ang ibang items.
Hindi naman. Mukhang lahat may relevance sa aged-friendly design.
Kapag ganito, usually ang tamang sagot ay iyong umbrella answer.

Ang trap dito ay mamimili ka ng pinaka-mukhang "technical," like grab bar, tapos makakalimutan mong broad ang tanong.

question: Given a 1.35m x 2.10m T&B, choose the nearest minimum size for window opening? (in meters)
keyMemory: Sa T&B window problems: Required area = floor area ÷ 20 Then choose the smallest option that still passes.

c. 0.50 x 0.50

Floor area ng T&B:
1.35 × 2.10 = 2.835 sq.m
Required window area:
2.835 ÷ 20 = 0.14175 sq.m
Ngayon, kuhanin natin ang area ng bawat choice:
• a. 0.36 × 0.60 = 0.216 sq.m
• b. 0.40 × 0.60 = 0.24 sq.m
• c. 0.50 × 0.50 = 0.25 sq.m
• d. 0.60 × 0.60 = 0.36 sq.m
Ang hinahanap ng tanong ay nearest minimum size.

Kaya letter a ang tamang sagot. (architectureboard.com.ph)

Ang tanong ay nearest minimum size.

pick the smallest option that still satisfies the code.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
b, c, d — all are acceptable in actual size because all exceed the minimum area.
Pero hindi sila ang nearest minimum.
Mas malaki sila kaysa kailangan.

Ito ang gusto kong matutunan mo:
Step 1: Compute required minimum area.
Step 2: Compute each option area.
Step 3: Huwag piliin ang "pinakamalaki" dahil lang safe siya.
Piliin ang smallest option that still complies.

Ang trap dito ay:
• Hindi ka magko-compute
• Pipili ka ng mukhang standard size like 0.60 × 0.60

question: Based on the existing regulations provided by the NBCP, how do you compute for the width of main exits in a building?

choices.text: not less than the total occupant load served divided by 165
At least 2 exits with each exit at a minimum width of 0.90 m
Based on the occupant loads of existing floor, plus 50% occupant load of upper floor and lower floor and 25% of the next adjacent floors

keyMemory: Sa exit-width computation, tandaan mo ang tatlong bagay: occupant load ÷ 165, plus adjacent-floor percentages, at minimum exit-doorway / minimum number of exits requirements. Sa reviewer framing na ito, ang sagot ay All of the above.
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"Based on the existing regulations provided by the NBCP, how do you compute for the width of main exits in a building?"
Choices as reconstructed from the split layout:
a. not less than the total occupant load served divided by 165
b. At least 2 exits with each exit at a minimum width of 0.90 m
c. Based on the occupant loads of existing floor, plus 50% occupant load of upper floor and lower floor and 25% of the next adjacent floors
d. All of the above
Correct answer: d. All of the above.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay All of the above.
Ito, medyo pangit lang ang pag-layout ng reviewer page kasi hati ang choices. Pero kapag binuo mo, makikita mo na ang intent ng tanong ay pagsamahin ang general width formula, minimum exit-doorway width, at how the required exit width from a storey is determined using adjacent-floor percentages. Sa NBCP, the total width of exits is occupant load served divided by 165, and the required width from a storey is based on the occupant load of that storey plus 50% of the first adjacent storey and 25% of the next adjacent storey that exits through that level. The code also requires exit doorways sized to permit a door not less than 900 mm wide, and floors above the first storey with occupant load of more than 10 must have at least two exits. Kaya sa framing ng reviewer, All of the above ang intended answer. (Google Sites)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Pansinin mo, hindi lang simpleng "ano ang formula?" ang reviewer item na ito. Pinagsama niya ang formula, distribution of load through levels, at minimum exit provision. Kaya kung isa-isa mong titingnan, may katotohanan ang a, may katotohanan ang b, at may katotohanan ang c. Iyon ang reason bakit ang tamang reviewer answer ay d. All of the above. (Google Sites)
Bakit hindi a lang?
Kasi totoo na occupant load ÷ 165 ang core width formula, pero hindi pa iyan ang buong story. Hindi pa nito sinasagot kung paano kino-compute ang width from a given storey when adjacent floors discharge through that level. Kaya kulang ang a kung iyon lang ang kukunin mo. (Google Sites)
Bakit hindi b lang?
Kasi ang 0.90 m refers to the minimum exit doorway size to permit installation of a door, and yes, required floors above the first storey with occupant load more than 10 generally need at least two exits. Pero hindi pa rin iyan ang full computation ng width. Kaya hindi sapat na b lang. (Google Sites)
Bakit hindi c lang?
Kasi ang c gives the storey-load adjustment method, pero wala pa rin doon ang main numerical formula na ÷165, kaya incomplete din siya if taken alone. (Google Sites)
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Kapag may tanong na "how do you compute" tapos maraming choices na mukhang lahat may kinalaman sa parehong rule, huwag agad mamili ng first formula na nakita mo. Tingnan mo kung may umbrella answer tulad ng "All of the above."
Kung ang a, b, at c ay hindi nagko-contradict at magkakaugnay talaga sa code topic, malaki ang chance na All of the above ang tama. Iyan mismo ang nangyari rito. (Google Sites)
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay dahil ang letter c ay naputol at hati sa dalawang columns, tapos maiinip ang examinee at pipili agad ng a dahil iyon ang pinaka-familiar na formula. Pero pag binuo mo ang buong item, makikita mong composite question siya.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa exit-width computation, tandaan mo ang tatlong bagay:
occupant load ÷ 165,
plus adjacent-floor percentages,
at minimum exit-doorway / minimum number of exits requirements.
Sa reviewer framing na ito, ang sagot ay All of the above.

question: What is the occupant load of a conference room?
keyMemory: Conference room = 1.40 sqm/person.
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"What is the occupant load of a conference room?"
Choices:
a. 1.40 sqm/person
b. 1.50 sqm/person
c. 1.60 sqm/person
d. 1.70 sqm/person
Correct answer: a. 1.40 sqm/person.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay 1.40 square meters per person.
Ito, direct occupant-load table item ito. Ang conference room ay kabilang sa less concentrated assembly use, kasama ng dining room, drinking establishment, exhibit room, gymnasium, or lounge, and the occupant load factor for that category is 1.4 sq.m/person. (Scribd)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Keyword dito: "conference room."
Kapag conference room, huwag mo siyang ihalo sa:
• auditorium or church na concentrated use without fixed seats, at
• standing room or waiting space.
Conference room belongs to the less concentrated use group, and that group is 1.4 sq.m/person. (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
1.50, 1.60, 1.70 — lahat iyan mukhang "pwede," pero wala sa standard occupant-load factor for conference room sa table na tina-test.
Ito ang type ng tanong na hindi mo dapat sagutin by gut feel. Hindi ito approximation problem. Exact table value ito. (Scribd)
Elimination method
Ito ang gusto kong isipin mo:
Kapag occupant-load table question, huwag kang manghula sa magkakalapit na decimal values.
Hanapin mo muna ang category:
• concentrated without fixed seats = 0.65
• less concentrated use like conference room = 1.4
• standing/waiting = 0.28
Kapag na-assign mo ang category, tapos na ang problem. (Scribd)
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay pinaghahalo ang conference room at classroom o ibang assembly types.
Tandaan mo: conference room is not the same as auditorium, and not the same as classroom.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Conference room = 1.40 sqm/person.

explanation:

"What is the occupant load for business offices?"

a. 2.80 sqm/person
b. 3.90 sqm/person
c. 5.60 sqm/person
d. 9.30 sqm/person
Correct answer: d. 9.30 sqm/person.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay 9.30 square meters per person.
Ito ay direct occupant-load table item din. Sa table shown on your page, Business offices = 9.3 sqm/person. The same figure also appears in reviewer and fire/egress references discussing office occupancy load factors. (Scribd)

Keyword dito: "business offices."
Huwag mo siyang ihalo sa:

Sa tanong na ito, ang category is plainly business offices, and the tested answer is 9.3 sqm/person. (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
2.80 sqm/person — trap ito dahil nasa same table family siya, pero hindi para sa business offices; that figure is associated with other occupancies such as street floor/below street floor mercantile spaces in the page table.
3.90 sqm/person — distractor lang, walang matching category dito.
5.60 sqm/person — another trap from the same table family, but not for business offices. (Scribd)

Ito ang board strategy:
Kapag occupant-load table ang tanong, bantayan mo kung ang choices ay mga totoong values from other categories. Dito, oo, marami sa choices ay familiar dahil galing sa parehong table.
So ang tamang galaw ay:
1. classify the occupancy first,
2. saka kunin ang exact factor.
Business office ba? Then 9.3 sqm/person. (Scribd)
Common trap sa board exam
Pinagpapalit ang business offices at call centers/BPO/open-plan concentrated offices.
Importanteng distinction iyan lalo na sa mga updated fire-code discussions. Pero sa item na nasa page mo, the intended tested value for business offices is 9.3 sqm/person. (Scribd)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Business offices = 9.30 sqm/person.
Sige, Page 14 tayo. May 6 visible questions dito. Dahan-dahan natin, at bantayan mo rito ang isang common ALE trap: maraming irrelevant-looking details ang isasama sa problem pero hindi lahat ginagamit sa sagot.

question: In a 15 storey high rise office building situated within a through lot with a building frontage of 20 meters facing a major road, and building depth of 15 meters. The rear of the lot faces a service road. The front and side has a setback of 8 meters and 5 meters respectively, what is the minimum number of fire exits?

choices.text: 2 main exits including the service stair
Provide 3 exits from ground to 8th floor and 2 exits from 9th to the 15th floor
Provide 2 exits excluding the service stairs

answer: A. 2 main exits including the service stair
keyMemory: Sa ganitong problem, occupant load muna bago site details. Dito, ang minimum ay 2 exits, at puwedeng included ang service stair.
explanation:
Basahin muna natin ang full question:
"In a 15 storey high rise office building situated within a through lot with a building frontage of 20 meters facing a major road, and building depth of 15 meters. The rear of the lot faces a service road. The front and side has a setback of 8 meters and 5 meters respectively, what is the minimum number of fire exits?"
Choices:
a. 2 main exits including the service stair
b. Provide 3 exits from ground to 8th floor and 2 exits from 9th to the 15th floor
c. Provide 2 exits excluding the service stairs
d. 4 exits from the ground up
Correct answer: a. 2 main exits including the service stair.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay 2 main exits including the service stair.
Ito ang tamang way mag-isip dito. Ang daming details sa problem — through lot, frontage, major road, service road, setbacks — pero ang pinaka-load-bearing clue ay office building at occupant load / number of exits. The office occupant-load factor used in reviewer materials is 9.3 sq.m/person, and the same reviewer-style exit table gives 2 exits for occupant loads from 50 to 500, 3 exits over 500, and 4 exits for 1000 or more. Using the page's own dimensions, 20 m × 15 m = 300 sq.m per floor, so that is about 32 persons per floor at 9.3 sq.m/person; whether you multiply that by 14 storeys as the reviewer did, or by 15 storeys, you still stay below 500 occupants, so the minimum remains 2 exits. (Scribd)
Ngayon, bakit letter a at hindi letter c? Kasi ang tanong ay minimum number of fire exits, hindi "ilang exits bukod sa service stair." Since the rear also faces a service road, puwedeng mag-function ang service stair as one of the required exits, so the two required exits can include that stair. Kaya a ang pinakatamang ALE answer. Yung c would effectively require 2 exits plus service stair, so higit na iyon sa minimum. (Scribd)
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Una, alisin ang mga detail na pampagulo lang. Hindi porke may major road at service road at setbacks, doon na iikot ang sagot.
Pangalawa, compute occupant load.
Pangatlo, compare sa exit table.
Kapag below 500, huwag kang papatok sa 3 exits o 4 exits.
Pagkatapos, tingnan kung alin sa 2-exit choices ang minimum. Dito, dahil puwedeng isama ang service stair, letter a ang natitira.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang common trap dito ay pinapansin mo ang through lot / setbacks tapos nakakalimutan mong occupant load ang primary basis ng exit count.
Isa pang trap: nakikita mo ang phrase na service stair at iniisip mong "hindi siguro kasama." Pero kung valid exit discharge siya, maaari siyang bilangin.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa ganitong problem, occupant load muna bago site details.
Dito, ang minimum ay 2 exits, at puwedeng included ang service stair.
