question: Based on BP 344 public transportation requirements, how many seats should be allotted for PWD on airplane?
answer: A. 2 seats on per aircraft-type basis
keyMemory: PWD seats in passenger airplanes = 2 per aircraft-type basis.
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"Based on BP 344 public transportation requirements, how many seats should be allotted for PWD on airplane?"
Choices:
a. 2 seats on per aircraft-type basis
b. 3 seats on per aircraft-type basis
c. 4 seats on per aircraft-type basis
d. 6 seats on per aircraft-type basis
Correct answer: a. 2 seats on per aircraft-type basis.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay 2 seats on a per aircraft-type basis.
Ito ay direct memory item. The BP 344 IRR states that passenger airplanes shall have at least two designated seats for disabled persons near the front exit/entrance door on a per aircraft-type basis. Kaya pag airplane ang tanong, huwag mong dalhin ang bus o train numbers. (National Council on Disability Affairs)
Bakit mali ang iba?
3, 4, 6 lahat puwedeng mukhang believable, pero hindi iyan ang exact BP 344 figure for airplanes. Ang 4 ay madalas mo maa-associate sa first class / premiere / air-conditioned buses, at ang 6 naman sa passenger trains per car. Kaya kung nalilito ka, ang gawin mo: ihiwalay mo ang transport modes sa utak mo. Airplane is 2. (National Council on Disability Affairs)
Elimination method
Kapag transportation seats ang tanong, gumawa ka agad sa isip mo ng mini-table:
• Airplane = 2
• First class / aircon bus = 4
• Regular bus = 5
• Passenger train = 6
Pag airplane ang lumabas, automatic 2.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
PWD seats in passenger airplanes = 2 per aircraft-type basis.
________________________________________

question: Based on BP344 public transportation requirements, how many seats should be allotted for PWD inside a first class bus?
keyMemory: First class / premiere / air-conditioned bus = 4 PWD seats near door.
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"Based on BP344 public transportation requirements, how many seats should be allotted for PWD inside a first class bus?"
Choices:
a. 2 seats near door
b. 3 seats near door
c. 4 seats near door
d. 6 near door
Correct answer: c. 4 seats near door.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay 4 seats near the door.
Ito rin ay direct BP 344 transportation provision. The IRR says first class, premiere and air-conditioned buses shall have at least four designated seats for disabled persons near the door. Kaya ang tamang sagot ay 4, hindi 2, hindi 3, hindi 6. (National Council on Disability Affairs)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
2 seats — iyan ang airplane number, hindi bus.
3 seats — filler distractor lang.
6 seats — iyan ang common train number, not first class bus.
Napapansin mo? Board exam loves to recycle real code numbers from other categories para magmukhang tama. Kaya dapat category-first lagi. (National Council on Disability Affairs)
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat:
"Anong mode of transport ito?"
Bus.
"Anong kind of bus?"
First class / premiere / air-conditioned.
Then recall the exact seat count: 4 near the door.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
First class / premiere / air-conditioned bus = 4 PWD seats near door.
________________________________________

question: Where would you locate rooms used for pre-schoolers, first grade and second grade pupils?

choices.text: At the floor of exit discharge and the floor above it
At the floor of exit discharge only
At the floor of exit discharge and the floor below it
At the floor of exit discharge and two floors above it

answer: B. At the floor of exit discharge only
keyMemory: For pre-schoolers, first grade, second grade, think floor of exit discharge only. (Scribd)
explanation:
Basahin muna natin ang full question:
"Where would you locate rooms used for pre-schoolers, first grade and second grade pupils?"
Choices:
a. At the floor of exit discharge and the floor above it
b. At the floor of exit discharge only
c. At the floor of exit discharge and the floor below it
d. At the floor of exit discharge and two floors above it
Correct answer: b. At the floor of exit discharge only.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay at the floor of exit discharge only.
Ito ang clue mo: pre-schoolers, first grade, second grade. Sa fire/life-safety logic, ito ang pinaka-vulnerable na users sa educational occupancy. Kaya hindi sila dapat ilagay sa taas o baba ng exit-discharge level. The available Fire Code reviewer sources consistently state that rooms used for preschoolers, first grade, and second grade pupils shall not be located below or above the floor of exit discharge. (Scribd)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Kapag ang rule says "shall not be located below or above the floor of exit discharge," ang ibig sabihin niyan, iisang level lang ang pwede: the floor of exit discharge itself. Kaya mali agad ang choices na may "floor above" o "floor below." (Scribd)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a mali kasi may floor above.
c mali kasi may floor below.
d mas lalo nang mali kasi two floors above na.
Common trap ito: baka isipin mo "pwede siguro one level above." Hindi. Ang wording ng rule mismo ang sumasara sa options na iyon. (Scribd)
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Kapag may phrase na "shall not be located below or above", hanapin mo ang choice na wala talagang below or above.
Sa apat na choices, si b lang ang natitira.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
For pre-schoolers, first grade, second grade, think floor of exit discharge only. (Scribd)

question: Where would you locate rooms used as emergency rooms, operating rooms, intensive care units, delivery rooms and other similar facilities?

choices.text: At the floor of exit discharge and 1 storey above it
At the floor of exit discharge only
At the floor of exit discharge and 1 floor below it
At the floor of exit discharge and 1 storey above and below it

answer: B. At the floor of exit discharge only
keyMemory: For ER, OR, ICU, delivery rooms, ang safest and current answer is level of exit discharge only. Pero tandaan mo rin na older reviewer references sometimes teach the one-storey-above-or-below exception. (Scribd)
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"Where would you locate rooms used as emergency rooms, operating rooms, intensive care units, delivery rooms and other similar facilities?"
Choices:
a. At the floor of exit discharge and 1 storey above it
b. At the floor of exit discharge only
c. At the floor of exit discharge and 1 floor below it
d. At the floor of exit discharge and 1 storey above and below it
Correct answer: b. At the floor of exit discharge only.
Uulitin ko: ang sagot ko rito ay at the floor of exit discharge only.
Ito ang isang tanong na gusto kong i-correct nang maayos. Older reviewer materials / older IRR references often mention that these facilities may be not more than one storey above or below the floor of exit discharge under certain conditions. Pero the newer revised Fire Code wording available in the 2019 IRR snippets points to these spaces being located on the level of exit discharge, then adds conditional provisions if that is not met. Dahil sinabi mo na Philippine standard first at dapat i-correct kapag may lumang reviewer wording, ang pipiliin ko rito ay b. (Scribd)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Keyword dito: emergency rooms, operating rooms, intensive care units, delivery rooms. Ito ang high-dependency spaces. Sa current life-safety logic, they should be on the level of exit discharge for faster evacuation and movement. Kaya ang most defensible current answer is b. (Scribd)
Bakit confusing ang choices?
Kasi maraming old reviewers ang nakabase sa older wording na one storey above or below under specific conditions. Iyon ang dahilan bakit mukhang plausible si d. Pero dahil ang tanong mo sa review natin ay dapat naka-prioritize sa mas current Philippine standard, mas tama ang b. (IIBH)
Elimination method
Ito ang ALE thinking:
Una, i-check kung old-reviewer trap ba ito.
Pangalawa, kapag may updated wording pointing to level of exit discharge, doon ka kakampi.
Kaya kahit tempting si d, ang mas tamang sagot ngayon ay b.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
For ER, OR, ICU, delivery rooms, ang safest and current answer is level of exit discharge only. Pero tandaan mo rin na older reviewer references sometimes teach the one-storey-above-or-below exception. (Scribd)
________________________________________

question: Basic function of an open door?
keyMemory: Kapag reviewer asks basic function of an open door, the expected answer is Means of Access. (Quizlet)
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"Basic function of an open door?"
Choices:
a. Fire Safety
b. Means of Access
c. Fire Exit
d. Horizontal Exit
Correct answer: b. Means of Access.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay Means of Access.
Ito, reviewer-style wording ito, hindi malinis na codal sentence. Pero sa ALE reviewer sets, this exact question is consistently answered as Means of Access. (Quizlet)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang pinto kapag bukas, ang pinaka-basic na function niya ay magbigay ng access or passage. Hindi pa agad "fire exit" ang general answer, kasi hindi lahat ng open door ay fire exit. Hindi rin "horizontal exit," dahil iyon ay specific means-of-egress term. Kaya ang pinakabasic at pinakageneral na tama ay Means of Access. (Quizlet)

question: As per Fire Code, what is the pipe diameter for the dry standpipe where the highest outlet is 23m or less above fire service connection?
keyMemory: Dry standpipe: 23 m or less = 102 mm; higher than 23 m = 153 mm. (Studocu)
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"As per Fire Code, what is the pipe diameter for the dry standpipe where the highest outlet is 23m or less above fire service connection?"
Choices:
a. 50 mm
b. 76 mm
c. 102 mm
d. 153 mm
Correct answer: c. 102 mm.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay 102 mm.
Ito, puwede mo ring makuha by pairing logic from the note on the page. Nakasulat sa page mo: 153 mm diameter where highest outlet is higher than 23 m above fire service connection. So the smaller category — 23 m or less — is the 102 mm dry standpipe size. Reviewer fire-code summaries also list 4 inches (102 mm) when the highest outlet is 23 m or less, and 6 inches (153 mm) when it is higher than 23 m. (Studocu)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat:
May threshold na 23 m.
Kapag higher than 23 m = 153 mm.
So kapag 23 m or less = 102 mm.
Kaya c ang sagot. (Studocu)
Bakit mali ang iba?
50 mm at 76 mm are too small for the dry standpipe main being asked.
153 mm is the category for higher than 23 m, not 23 m or less.
Elimination method
Kapag may paired standpipe sizes:
• 102 mm for lower range
• 153 mm for higher range
Huwag lang basta kabisaduhin ang number. Kabisaduhin mo ang threshold.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Dry standpipe: 23 m or less = 102 mm; higher than 23 m = 153 mm. (Studocu)

answer: B. By the volume of pedestrians who will use the sidewalk on a regular basis
keyMemory: Sidewalk width is determined primarily by pedestrian volume. (Department of Public Works and Highways)
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"How do you determine the width of a sidewalk?"
Choices:
a. By the width of a planting strip
b. By the volume of pedestrians who will use the sidewalk on a regular basis
c. By the assigned Zoning of the area
d. By the width of the adjacent carriageway
Correct answer: b. By the volume of pedestrians who will use the sidewalk on a regular basis.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay by the volume of pedestrians who will use the sidewalk on a regular basis.
Ito ay planning logic question. Hindi planting strip ang primary determinant. Hindi rin zoning label lang, at lalong hindi simpleng carriageway width. Sidewalk width is fundamentally determined by pedestrian demand or pedestrian volume. That is the sound planning basis in walkability and sidewalk design. (Department of Public Works and Highways)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang sidewalk is for pedestrian movement.
So the first design basis mo dapat is ilan ang gumagamit at gaano kadalas.
That makes pedestrian volume the most defensible answer among the choices. (Department of Public Works and Highways)
Bakit mali ang iba?
Planting strip width is secondary design component lang.
Assigned zoning may influence context, but not the direct sizing logic asked here.
Adjacent carriageway width is road-related, not the primary pedestrian-capacity basis.
Elimination method
Ito ang tanong mo sa sarili mo:
"Para saan ba ang sidewalk?"
Kung para sa tao, then the width should be based on pedestrian use, not on trees, cars, or zoning label alone.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sidewalk width is determined primarily by pedestrian volume. (Department of Public Works and Highways)

question: If a river is located in a highly urbanized area, what should be the minimum easement required per side of its waterway?
keyMemory: Water Code easement rule: urban = 3 m, agricultural = 20 m, forest = 40 m. (water.emb.gov.ph)
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"If a river is located in a highly urbanized area, what should be the minimum easement required per side of its waterway?"
Choices:
a. 1.50 m each side
b. 10.00 m each side
c. 3.00 m each side
d. 20.00 m each side
Correct answer: c. 3.00 m each side.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay 3.00 meters each side.
Ito ay direct Water Code rule. The banks of rivers and streams in urban areas are subject to a three-meter easement along their margins. Since the question says highly urbanized area, the matching category is still the urban-area easement: 3 meters. (Lawphil)
Bakit hindi 10 m?
That is the Metro Manila/Pasig River-system reviewer trap from special MMDA provisions. But the question here is phrased as a river in a highly urbanized area in general, so the baseline Water Code answer is 3.00 m each side. (water.emb.gov.ph)
Elimination method: tandaan mo ang classic 3-20-40 rule:
• 3 m urban
• 20 m agricultural
• 40 m forest
Kapag urban ang clue, automatic 3 m.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Water Code easement rule: urban = 3 m, agricultural = 20 m, forest = 40 m. (water.emb.gov.ph)

question: This is a document issued by DENR that certifies that based on the representations of the proponent, the proposed project or undertaking WILL NOT cause significant negative environmental impact?
keyMemory: EIA = process, EIS = study/document, ECA = area, ECC = certificate issued by DENR. (Energy Information Administration) Sige, last page na tayo. Isa na lang ang visible question dito, so tapusin natin nang maayos.
explanation:
Basahin natin ang full question:
"This is a document issued by DENR that certifies that based on the representations of the proponent, the proposed project or undertaking WILL NOT cause significant negative environmental impact?"
Choices:
a. EIA
b. EIS
c. ECC
d. ECA
Correct answer: c. ECC.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay ECC, or Environmental Compliance Certificate.
Ito ay very direct official definition item. DENR/EMB materials define an ECC as the document issued after a positive review, certifying that based on the proponent's representations, the project or undertaking will not cause significant negative environmental impact. (DENR NCR)
Bakit mali ang iba?
EIA is the Environmental Impact Assessment process.
EIS is the Environmental Impact Statement document.
ECA means Environmentally Critical Area.
Ang ini-issue na certificate ng DENR is ECC. (Energy Information Administration)
Elimination method: kapag tinanong ka ng "what is the document issued by DENR?", hanapin mo ang certificate, not the study, not the process, not the area classification.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
EIA = process, EIS = study/document, ECA = area, ECC = certificate issued by DENR. (Energy Information Administration)
Sige, last page na tayo. Isa na lang ang visible question dito, so tapusin natin nang maayos.

question: It refers to a cultural property found locally which is one hundred (100) years in age, more or less; the production of which has ceased.
keyMemory: Kapag ang clue ay 100 years old, locally found cultural property, no longer in production, ang sagot mo agad ay Antique. Tapos na tayo sa buong visible set. Congrats — natapos natin hanggang dulo.
explanation:
Basahin muna natin ang full question:
"It refers to a cultural property found locally which is one hundred (100) years in age, more or less; the production of which has ceased."
Choices:
a. Cultural Heritage
b. Antique
c. Cultural Markers
d. Historical shrines
Correct answer: b. Antique.
Uulitin ko: ang tamang sagot ay Antique.
Ito, direct definition question ito. At maganda ito kasi hindi ito hula-hula. Sa RA 10066 or National Cultural Heritage Act, the law defines "Antique" as a cultural property found locally which is one hundred years in age, more or less, and the production of which has ceased. Exact phrase iyan ng batas. (Lawphil)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Pansinin mo ang clue words:
• cultural property found locally
• 100 years in age, more or less
• production has ceased
Kapag ganyan ang wording, hindi ito general "heritage" lang. Hindi rin marker o shrine.
Iyan ay very specific legal definition ng Antique. (Lawphil)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Cultural Heritage — masyado itong broad.
Ang cultural heritage ay umbrella concept. Maraming klase ng cultural properties ang sakop niyan. Pero ang tanong humihingi ng specific term with a very exact legal definition. Kaya masyadong malawak si Cultural Heritage para maging tama. (Lawphil)
c. Cultural Markers — mali ito kasi marker ito, not the object/property itself.
Ang cultural marker is usually a plaque or official marker placed to identify significance. Hindi iyan ang tawag sa 100-year-old locally found cultural property na hindi na ginagawa. (Lawphil)
d. Historical shrines — mali rin kasi shrine is a specific historical site/place classification.
Hindi iyan object-definition question. Ang tanong ay tumutukoy sa isang class of cultural property, not a shrine. (Lawphil)
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
Step 1: Hanapin kung broad ba o specific ang tanong.
Dito, sobrang specific ng description.
Step 2: Tanggalin ang broad umbrella term.
So alisin mo agad si Cultural Heritage.
Step 3: Tanggalin ang choices na site/marker type, not property-definition type.
So tanggal si Cultural Markers at Historical shrines.
Step 4: Tingnan ang natira.
Antique na lang ang eksaktong legal term na tumutugma sa lahat ng clue.
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot
Sabihin mo sa isip mo:
"Specific legal definition ito. Hindi ito category in general. Hindi ito place. Hindi ito marker. Object/property term ito."
Kapag ganoon ang mindset mo, mabilis mong makukuha ang Antique.
Common trap sa board exam
Ang trap dito ay pipiliin mo ang Cultural Heritage dahil parang mas sosyal o mas formal pakinggan.
Pero board exam logic is not about the nicest-sounding term.
It is about the exact statutory definition.
Philippine basis
RA 10066, National Cultural Heritage Act, defines Antique as a cultural property found locally which is one hundred years in age, more or less, and the production of which has ceased. (Lawphil)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang clue ay 100 years old, locally found cultural property, no longer in production, ang sagot mo agad ay Antique.
Tapos na tayo sa buong visible set. Congrats — natapos natin hanggang dulo.
